model.name: Plant Disease Detection Model V3 - Extended
model.description: ResNet50-based plant disease detection covering 57 classes across 14+ plant species

classes.name: Apple Scab
symptoms: Olive-green to dark brown spots on leaves
Velvety or sooty texture on leaf surface
Distorted or curled leaves
Dark, scabby lesions on fruit
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Venturia inaequalis, spread by wind and rain in cool, wet weather

treatment.chemical: Apply captan or myclobutanil fungicides during early spring
treatment.cultural: Rake and destroy fallen leaves, prune for better air circulation
treatment.preventive: Plant scab-resistant apple varieties, maintain orchard sanitation

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks during growing season
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit quality and yield by 20-40% if untreated

classes.name: Black Rot
symptoms: Purple or reddish-brown spots on leaves with concentric rings
Frogeye leaf spot pattern
Black, shriveled mummified fruit
Cankers on branches with rough, cracked bark
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa, overwinters in mummified fruit and cankers

treatment.chemical: Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicides at petal fall
treatment.cultural: Remove mummified fruit, prune cankers, destroy infected debris
treatment.preventive: Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization, remove dead wood promptly

classes.urgency: High - begin treatment immediately to prevent spread
classes.economic_impact: Can cause significant crop loss of 30-50% in severe cases

classes.name: Cedar Apple Rust
symptoms: Bright yellow-orange spots on upper leaf surface
Cup-shaped structures on leaf undersides
Premature leaf drop in severe cases
Spots may appear on fruit surface
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae, requires both apple and cedar/juniper hosts

treatment.chemical: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb fungicides from pink bud stage
treatment.cultural: Remove nearby juniper or cedar trees within 2 miles if possible
treatment.preventive: Plant rust-resistant apple varieties, monitor for early symptoms

classes.urgency: Medium - treat during spring infection period
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 10-25% and affect fruit appearance

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy apple plant with vibrant green leaves
No visible disease symptoms
Normal growth pattern and fruit development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care, pruning, and monitoring
treatment.preventive: Maintain good orchard hygiene and balanced nutrition

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy blueberry plant with vibrant foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal berry development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care and acidic soil maintenance
treatment.preventive: Maintain proper soil pH (4.5-5.5) and adequate mulching

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves and shoots
Leaf curling and distortion
Stunted new growth
Premature leaf drop
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Podosphaera clandestina, thrives in warm, dry conditions with poor air circulation

treatment.chemical: Apply sulfur-based or triazole fungicides at first sign of infection
treatment.cultural: Prune for better air circulation, remove infected shoots
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, maintain tree spacing

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit quality and yield by 15-30%

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy cherry plant with clean foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal fruit development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care and monitoring
treatment.preventive: Maintain proper pruning and orchard hygiene

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Common Rust
symptoms: Small reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
Pustules turn dark brown to black as they mature
Heavy infection causes premature leaf death
Reduced ear fill and grain quality
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Puccinia sorghi, spores spread by wind from southern regions

treatment.chemical: Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicides if detected before tasseling
treatment.cultural: Plant early to avoid peak spore dispersal periods
treatment.preventive: Use rust-resistant corn hybrids, monitor fields regularly

classes.urgency: Medium - treat before tasseling for best results
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 10-30% in susceptible hybrids

classes.name: Gray Leaf Spot
symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions between leaf veins
Lesions become gray and opaque over time
Lower leaves affected first, progressing upward
Severe infection can blight entire leaves
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis, favored by warm humid conditions and continuous corn

treatment.chemical: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides at VT-R1 growth stage
treatment.cultural: Practice crop rotation, reduce corn residue with tillage
treatment.preventive: Select resistant hybrids, avoid continuous corn planting

classes.urgency: High - treat before or at tasseling stage
classes.economic_impact: Can cause yield losses of 20-50% in severe epidemics

classes.name: Common Rust
symptoms: Small reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
Pustules darken as they mature
Premature leaf death in severe cases
Reduced ear fill and grain quality
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Puccinia sorghi, spores carried by wind over long distances

treatment.chemical: Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicides if detected before tasseling
treatment.cultural: Plant early to avoid peak infection periods
treatment.preventive: Use rust-resistant hybrids, scout fields regularly

classes.urgency: Medium - treat before tasseling for best results
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 10-30% in susceptible hybrids

classes.name: Northern Leaf Blight
symptoms: Large cigar-shaped gray-green lesions on leaves
Lesions expand and can coalesce to blight entire leaves
Lower leaves usually infected first
Dark spore production visible on lesions in humid weather
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Exserohilum turcicum, favored by moderate temperatures and heavy dew

treatment.chemical: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides at first sign near tasseling
treatment.cultural: Practice crop rotation with non-host crops, manage residue
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant hybrids with Ht genes, avoid continuous corn

classes.urgency: High - treat promptly when lesions appear before tasseling
classes.economic_impact: Can cause yield losses of 30-50% if infection occurs before silking

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy corn plant with vigorous growth
No visible disease symptoms on leaves
Normal ear development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular crop management practices
treatment.preventive: Maintain proper fertilization and irrigation scheduling

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Corn Blight
symptoms: Large spreading lesions on corn leaves
Rapid leaf death and drying
Reduced photosynthetic capacity
Stalk weakness and lodging in severe cases
classes.causes: Fungal infection, often Helminthosporium or Bipolaris species, favored by warm, wet conditions

treatment.chemical: Apply broad-spectrum foliar fungicides at first sign of infection
treatment.cultural: Remove crop residue, practice crop rotation with non-grasses
treatment.preventive: Use blight-resistant hybrids, avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization

classes.urgency: High - treat immediately to prevent rapid spread
classes.economic_impact: Can cause devastating yield losses of 30-70% in epidemics

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy corn plant with strong green foliage
No visible disease or pest symptoms
Normal tassel and ear development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue good agricultural practices
treatment.preventive: Maintain soil health and balanced nutrition

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Black Rot
symptoms: Small brown circular spots on leaves with dark borders
Black elongated cankers on shoots
Berries shrivel and turn into hard black mummies
Rapid fruit destruction in humid conditions
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Guignardia bidwellii, overwinters in mummified berries

treatment.chemical: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil from bud break through veraison
treatment.cultural: Remove mummified berries and infected canes immediately
treatment.preventive: Maintain open canopy with proper pruning, ensure good air circulation

classes.urgency: High - begin treatment at bud break and maintain through season
classes.economic_impact: Can destroy 50-80% of the fruit crop in severe outbreaks

classes.name: Esca (Black Measles)
symptoms: Dark spots and streaks on berries resembling measles
Tiger-striped discoloration on leaves
Sudden wilting and death of whole vine (apoplexy)
Internal wood shows dark streaking and decay
classes.causes: Complex of wood-inhabiting fungi including Phaeomoniella chlamydospora and Phaeoacremonium

treatment.chemical: No effective chemical cure; trunk injection with fungicides may help
treatment.cultural: Retrain vines from suckers, remove severely infected vines
treatment.preventive: Protect pruning wounds with wound sealants, delay pruning in wet weather

classes.urgency: High - manage promptly for vine longevity
classes.economic_impact: Major economic threat; can shorten vineyard lifespan significantly

classes.name: Leaf Blight (Isariopsis)
symptoms: Dark brown to black angular spots on leaves
Spots may merge to form larger blighted areas
Premature defoliation affecting vine vigor
Reduced sugar content in fruit
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Isariopsis species, favored by humid conditions

treatment.chemical: Apply copper-based or mancozeb fungicides preventively
treatment.cultural: Improve air circulation through canopy management
treatment.preventive: Avoid overhead irrigation, maintain proper vine spacing

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce wine quality and yield by 15-30%

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy grapevine with clean foliage
No visible disease or pest damage
Normal fruit set and ripening
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular vineyard management
treatment.preventive: Maintain canopy management and balanced nutrition

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Gray Leaf Spot
symptoms: Rectangular gray-brown lesions parallel to leaf veins
Lesions have sharp, distinct margins
Lower leaves infected first, spreading upward
Severe cases cause complete leaf blight
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis, thrives in warm, humid environments

treatment.chemical: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides between V6 and VT stages
treatment.cultural: Rotate crops with non-host species, manage residue through tillage
treatment.preventive: Use resistant hybrids, avoid continuous corn in humid regions

classes.urgency: High - treat before or at tasseling for maximum benefit
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yields by 20-50% in susceptible fields

classes.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Angular yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Purplish-gray downy growth on leaf undersides
Rapid leaf browning and defoliation
Fruit sunscald due to loss of leaf canopy
classes.causes: Oomycete infection caused by Pseudoperonospora cubensis, spread by wind-borne spores in wet conditions

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or mandipropamid fungicides at first symptom appearance
treatment.cultural: Improve air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, use fungicide spray schedule in humid regions

classes.urgency: High - treat immediately as disease spreads very rapidly
classes.economic_impact: Can cause total crop loss in severe epidemics

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy muskmelon plant with vigorous vines
No visible disease or nutrient deficiency symptoms
Normal fruit set and development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular watering and vine training
treatment.preventive: Maintain proper spacing and soil nutrition

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves, stems, and petioles
Leaves turn yellow and dry out
Premature leaf drop reducing fruit quality
Reduced sugar content and flavor in fruit
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Podosphaera xanthii or Erysiphe cichoracearum

treatment.chemical: Apply sulfur-based or potassium bicarbonate fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove severely infected leaves, avoid dense planting
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, ensure good air circulation between plants

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1 week of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit quality and yield by 20-40%

classes.name: Calcium Deficiency
symptoms: Blossom end rot on fruit
Distorted and curled new growth
Brown necrotic spots on leaf margins
Weak stems and poor fruit development
classes.causes: Insufficient calcium uptake due to irregular watering, low soil calcium, or high nitrogen/potassium levels

treatment.chemical: Apply calcium chloride or calcium nitrate foliar spray
treatment.cultural: Ensure consistent watering schedule, mulch to retain soil moisture
treatment.preventive: Test and amend soil pH (6.0-6.8), add gypsum or lime before planting

classes.urgency: Medium - address within 1-2 weeks to prevent fruit loss
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce marketable fruit by 20-40%

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy muskmelon plant
No visible disease or deficiency symptoms
Normal growth and fruit development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care and monitoring
treatment.preventive: Maintain balanced nutrition and proper irrigation

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Nitrogen Deficiency
symptoms: Uniform yellowing of older leaves (chlorosis)
Stunted plant growth and reduced vine vigor
Smaller than normal leaves
Reduced fruit size and yield
classes.causes: Insufficient soil nitrogen, poor fertilization, or leaching from heavy rainfall/irrigation

treatment.chemical: Apply urea or ammonium nitrate fertilizer as side-dress
treatment.cultural: Add compost or well-rotted manure, use nitrogen-fixing cover crops
treatment.preventive: Follow soil test recommendations, split nitrogen applications through the season

classes.urgency: Medium - address within 1-2 weeks for growing plants
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 25-50% if uncorrected

classes.name: Potassium Deficiency
symptoms: Brown scorching and curling of leaf edges
Interveinal chlorosis on older leaves
Weak stems prone to breakage
Poor fruit quality and reduced sugar content
classes.causes: Insufficient potassium in soil, sandy or highly leached soils, or antagonism from excess calcium/magnesium

treatment.chemical: Apply potassium sulfate or muriate of potash fertilizer
treatment.cultural: Add wood ash or compost rich in potassium
treatment.preventive: Conduct soil tests before planting, apply potassium based on crop requirements

classes.urgency: Medium - address within 1-2 weeks for fruit quality
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit quality and marketability by 20-35%

classes.name: Huanglongbing (Citrus Greening)
symptoms: Asymmetric blotchy yellowing of leaves (mottling)
Lopsided, small, and bitter fruit that stays green
Twig dieback and stunted growth
Premature fruit drop
classes.causes: Bacterial infection (Candidatus Liberibacter) transmitted by Asian citrus psyllid (Diaphorina citri)

treatment.chemical: No cure exists; manage psyllid vectors with systemic insecticides like imidacloprid
treatment.cultural: Remove infected trees to reduce inoculum, maintain tree nutrition to prolong productivity
treatment.preventive: Control psyllid populations aggressively, use certified disease-free nursery stock

classes.urgency: Critical - no cure, manage immediately to slow spread
classes.economic_impact: Devastating - can destroy entire citrus groves within 5-8 years

classes.name: Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small angular water-soaked spots on leaves
Spots turn brown with yellow halos
Shot-hole appearance as lesion centers fall out
Pit-like lesions on fruit surface
classes.causes: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni, spread by rain splash and wind

treatment.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericides during dormant and early season
treatment.cultural: Prune for air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, maintain balanced nutrition

classes.urgency: Medium - treat preventively from petal fall onward
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit quality and grade by 20-40%

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy peach tree with clean foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal fruit development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular pruning and care
treatment.preventive: Maintain dormant sprays and orchard hygiene

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit
Spots enlarge and become brown with raised edges
Severe defoliation in wet conditions
Scabby lesions on fruit reducing marketability
classes.causes: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas species, spread by rain splash and contaminated seed

treatment.chemical: Apply copper hydroxide mixed with mancozeb fungicide
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plant debris, avoid working with wet plants
treatment.preventive: Use certified disease-free seed, practice crop rotation

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 10-30% and fruit marketability

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy pepper plant with vibrant green foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal fruit set and development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care and monitoring
treatment.preventive: Maintain good plant hygiene and proper growing conditions

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Test Sample
symptoms: Image from PlantVillage test dataset
May show various plant conditions
Requires manual verification
classes.causes: Test dataset sample - classification may not be reliable for this category

treatment.chemical: Consult an agricultural expert for proper diagnosis
treatment.cultural: Take additional photos and submit for re-analysis
treatment.preventive: Monitor plant closely for developing symptoms

classes.urgency: Low - verify with expert consultation
classes.economic_impact: Variable - depends on actual condition

classes.name: Early Blight
symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring lesions (target spots) on leaves
Lesions start on lower, older leaves
Yellowing around lesion margins
Premature defoliation reducing tuber size
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Alternaria solani, favored by warm temperatures and alternating wet/dry conditions

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plant debris, improve air circulation
treatment.preventive: Avoid overhead watering, plant resistant varieties, practice crop rotation

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce tuber yield by 20-40% if untreated

classes.name: Late Blight
symptoms: Large water-soaked dark green to brown lesions on leaves
White fuzzy sporulation on leaf undersides in humid conditions
Rapid spread causing entire plant collapse
Brown rot in tubers
classes.causes: Oomycete infection caused by Phytophthora infestans, historically caused the Irish Potato Famine

treatment.chemical: Apply mancozeb or metalaxyl-based fungicides immediately upon detection
treatment.cultural: Destroy infected plants and cull piles, avoid irrigation during outbreaks
treatment.preventive: Use certified seed, plant resistant varieties, monitor weather forecasts

classes.urgency: Critical - treat immediately; can destroy entire crop in days
classes.economic_impact: Can cause total crop loss within 7-10 days in favorable conditions

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy potato plant with normal foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal growth pattern and tuber development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular hilling and care
treatment.preventive: Maintain good seed selection and crop rotation

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy raspberry plant with vigorous canes
No visible disease symptoms
Normal fruit production
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular pruning of spent canes
treatment.preventive: Maintain good air circulation and weed control

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Bacterial Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions along leaf margins turning yellow then white
Wilting and rolling of young leaves (kresek phase)
Milky or yellow bacterial ooze from cut leaves
Pale yellow leaves that dry rapidly
classes.causes: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae, spread by wind, rain, and contaminated water

treatment.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericides; limited chemical options
treatment.cultural: Drain fields periodically, avoid excess nitrogen, remove infected stubble
treatment.preventive: Use resistant rice varieties, treat seed with hot water, balanced fertilization

classes.urgency: High - act within first week of symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 20-50% in severe outbreaks

classes.name: Brown Spot
symptoms: Oval brown spots with gray centers on leaves
Spots may appear on leaf sheaths and grain husks
Seedling blight in nurseries
Reduced grain quality and discolored hulls
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Bipolaris oryzae, favored by nutrient-deficient soils and water stress

treatment.chemical: Apply propiconazole or iprodione fungicides at boot stage
treatment.cultural: Improve soil fertility, especially potassium and silicon levels
treatment.preventive: Use healthy certified seed, balance soil nutrition, manage water levels

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 15-40% and downgrade grain quality

classes.name: Leaf Smut
symptoms: Small angular black spots or smut-filled lesions on leaves
Black powdery spore masses visible on leaf surface
Affected leaves may twist slightly
Usually a minor disease with limited economic damage
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Entyloma oryzae, favored by high nitrogen and humid conditions

treatment.chemical: Generally not needed; apply foliar fungicides only in severe cases
treatment.cultural: Reduce excessive nitrogen application, maintain field drainage
treatment.preventive: Use balanced fertilization, avoid late-season nitrogen top-dressing

classes.urgency: Low - monitor; treatment rarely needed
classes.economic_impact: Minimal impact; yield losses typically less than 5%

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy soybean plant with normal trifoliate leaves
No visible disease or pest damage
Normal pod development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular crop management practices
treatment.preventive: Practice proper crop rotation and seed treatment

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery spots on leaf surfaces
Leaves yellow, brown, and die prematurely
Reduced fruit size and quality
Stems and petioles also affected
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Podosphaera xanthii, spread by airborne spores in warm, dry conditions

treatment.chemical: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove severely infected leaves, maintain plant spacing
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, ensure good air circulation

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1 week of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit yield and quality by 20-40%

classes.name: Leaf Scorch
symptoms: Small dark purple spots that enlarge and merge
Leaf margins turn brown and appear scorched
Reduced plant vigor and runner production
Petioles and calyxes may also be affected
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Diplocarpon earlianum, spread by rain splash and overhead irrigation

treatment.chemical: Apply copper-based or captan fungicides after harvest
treatment.cultural: Remove old leaves after harvest, improve drainage
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, ensure proper spacing

classes.urgency: Medium - treat after harvest to protect next season
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 15-30% if severe infection persists

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy strawberry plant with clean foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal runner and fruit production
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular care, mulching, and renovation
treatment.preventive: Maintain bed hygiene and proper watering practices

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Bacterial Spot
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit
Spots turn dark brown with yellow halos
Severe defoliation in wet weather
Raised scabby lesions on fruit
classes.causes: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas species, spread by rain splash and contaminated seed

treatment.chemical: Apply copper hydroxide mixed with mancozeb at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plant debris, avoid overhead irrigation
treatment.preventive: Use certified disease-free seed, rotate crops, space plants properly

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 10-30% and fruit marketability

classes.name: Early Blight
symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring (target) spots on lower leaves
Yellow halos around lesions
Progressive defoliation from bottom up
Fruit may develop dark leathery spots near stem end
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Alternaria solani, overwinters in plant debris and soil

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove lower leaves touching soil, stake plants for air flow
treatment.preventive: Mulch around plants, practice 3-year crop rotation, use resistant varieties

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of symptom onset
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 20-40% through premature defoliation

classes.name: Late Blight
symptoms: Large irregularly shaped water-soaked gray-green spots
White fuzzy growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions
Rapid browning and collapse of entire plant
Brown firm rot on fruit
classes.causes: Oomycete infection caused by Phytophthora infestans, spreads explosively in cool, wet weather

treatment.chemical: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil immediately; copper-based for organic
treatment.cultural: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately; do not compost
treatment.preventive: Monitor late blight forecasts, use certified transplants, provide air circulation

classes.urgency: Critical - treat within 24-48 hours to prevent total loss
classes.economic_impact: Can destroy entire crop within 7-10 days in favorable conditions

classes.name: Leaf Mold
symptoms: Yellowish green spots on upper leaf surface
Olive-green to brown velvety mold on leaf undersides
Leaves curl, wither, and drop
Common in greenhouse and high-tunnel production
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Passalora fulva (Cladosporium fulvum), thrives in high humidity

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Improve ventilation, reduce humidity below 85%, space plants wider
treatment.preventive: Use resistant varieties, ensure greenhouse ventilation, avoid leaf wetness

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1 week, especially in greenhouses
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce greenhouse yields by 20-40%

classes.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small circular spots with dark borders and gray centers
Tiny dark specks (pycnidia) visible in spot centers
Progressive defoliation starting from lower leaves
Plants become spindly and fruits sunburned
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Septoria lycopersici, spread by rain splash from infected debris

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove lower infected leaves, mulch around plants
treatment.preventive: Practice crop rotation, stake plants, avoid overhead watering

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks to prevent defoliation
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 30-50% through severe defoliation

classes.name: Two-Spotted Spider Mite
symptoms: Tiny yellow-white stippling on leaf upper surface
Fine webbing on leaf undersides and between branches
Leaves turn bronze and dry out
Mites visible as tiny moving dots on leaf undersides
classes.causes: Infestation by Tetranychus urticae, thrives in hot and dry conditions, often worse with pesticide overuse

treatment.chemical: Apply miticides like abamectin or spiromesifen; avoid broad-spectrum insecticides
treatment.cultural: Spray plants with strong water stream to dislodge mites, increase humidity
treatment.preventive: Encourage natural predators (Phytoseiulus persimilis), avoid dusty conditions

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1 week as populations explode quickly
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 20-40% and cause premature plant death

classes.name: Target Spot
symptoms: Small brown spots with concentric rings on leaves
Spots may appear on stems and fruit
Lower leaves affected first
Severe defoliation in warm humid conditions
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Corynespora cassiicola, favored by warm humid environments

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin fungicides preventively
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plant debris, improve air circulation
treatment.preventive: Use pathogen-free seed, practice crop rotation, avoid overhead watering

classes.urgency: Medium - treat within 1-2 weeks of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 15-30% through defoliation

classes.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
symptoms: Severe upward curling and cupping of leaves
Yellowing of leaf margins
Stunted, bushy growth with reduced internodes
Drastically reduced or no fruit production
classes.causes: Viral infection transmitted persistently by silverleaf whitefly (Bemisia tabaci)

treatment.chemical: No cure; control whitefly vectors with imidacloprid or pyriproxyfen
treatment.cultural: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately, use reflective mulches
treatment.preventive: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties, install insect-proof screens, control whiteflies early

classes.urgency: Critical - remove infected plants immediately to prevent spread
classes.economic_impact: Can cause 80-100% yield loss; plants infected early produce no fruit

classes.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves
Leaf distortion, curling, and fern-leaf symptoms
Stunted plant growth
Fruit mottling, internal browning, and reduced quality
classes.causes: Tobacco Mosaic Virus (ToMV), extremely stable virus spread by contaminated hands, tools, and seed

treatment.chemical: No chemical cure exists for viral diseases
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plants, disinfect tools with 10% bleach or milk solution
treatment.preventive: Use TMV-resistant varieties, wash hands before handling plants, avoid tobacco products near plants

classes.urgency: High - remove infected plants immediately, disinfect all tools
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce yield by 20-50% depending on time of infection

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy tomato plant with vibrant green foliage
No visible disease symptoms
Normal growth pattern and fruit development
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular staking, pruning, and watering
treatment.preventive: Maintain good garden hygiene and balanced fertilization

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Dark brown to black sunken lesions on leaves, stems, and fruit
Pink-orange spore masses in wet conditions
Leaf spots may have concentric rings
Fruit rot with deep sunken cavities
classes.causes: Fungal infection caused by Colletotrichum orbiculare, favored by warm temperatures and frequent rainfall

treatment.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or azoxystrobin fungicides at first sign
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plant debris, avoid working with wet plants
treatment.preventive: Use resistant varieties, practice 2-3 year crop rotation, use drip irrigation

classes.urgency: High - treat immediately to prevent fruit loss
classes.economic_impact: Can cause 30-60% crop loss in severe epidemics

classes.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Angular yellow spots on upper leaf surface bound by veins
Purplish-gray fuzzy growth on leaf undersides
Rapid leaf browning and death
Fruit sunscald due to canopy loss
classes.causes: Oomycete infection caused by Pseudoperonospora cubensis, spread by wind-borne sporangia

treatment.chemical: Apply mandipropamid or fluopicolide fungicides at first detection
treatment.cultural: Improve air circulation, remove infected leaves promptly
treatment.preventive: Monitor forecasting systems, apply preventive fungicides during high-risk periods

classes.urgency: High - treat within 24-48 hours of first symptoms
classes.economic_impact: Can cause rapid defoliation leading to 40-80% yield loss

classes.name: Healthy
symptoms: Healthy watermelon plant with vigorous vine growth
No visible disease symptoms
Normal fruit development and ripening
classes.causes: No disease present

treatment.chemical: No treatment needed
treatment.cultural: Continue regular irrigation and vine management
treatment.preventive: Maintain proper spacing and soil nutrition

classes.urgency: None - plant is healthy
classes.economic_impact: No negative impact

classes.name: Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Light and dark green mottled pattern on leaves
Leaf distortion, blistering, and reduced size
Fruit mottling with irregular bumps
Stunted vine growth and reduced fruit set
classes.causes: Watermelon Mosaic Virus (WMV) or Papaya Ringspot Virus (PRSV), transmitted by aphids

treatment.chemical: No chemical cure; control aphid vectors with insecticidal soaps or neem oil
treatment.cultural: Remove infected plants immediately, control weeds that harbor aphids
treatment.preventive: Use virus-resistant varieties, reflective mulches to repel aphids, row covers early season

classes.urgency: High - remove infected plants to prevent spread to healthy vines
classes.economic_impact: Can reduce fruit marketability and yield by 30-70%

none.description: No disease detected - plant appears healthy

very_high.description: Critical infection - aggressive treatment needed immediately

chemical.name: Chemical Treatment
chemical.description: Fungicides, bactericides, and other chemical interventions
